title: lappula lappula
intro: Lappula lappula, also known as Lappula, is a perennial plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) native to Eastern Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone and flavonoid content. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Lappula lappula can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and other respiratory issues.
